title: Droid Razr M

infobox.value: 1.5 GHz Dual-core [Qualcomm](/wiki/Qualcomm "Qualcomm") [Snapdragon](/wiki/Snapdragon_(system_on_chip) "Snapdragon (system on chip)") S4 MSM8960[[1]](#cite_note-engadget1-1) [Intel Atom](/wiki/Intel_Atom "Intel Atom") Z2460 Razr i

8MP, Sony [Exmor](/wiki/Exmor "Exmor") IMX145 List   * 8.0 [Megapixels](/wiki/Megapixel "Megapixel") * [HD video](/wiki/High-definition_video "High-definition video") ([1080p](/wiki/1080p "1080p")) at 30 [FPS](/wiki/Frame_rate "Frame rate")[[1]](#cite_note-engadget1-1)
4.3″(10.9cm) edge-to-edge 960x540[qHD](/wiki/540p "540p") (256 ppi) [Super AMOLED Advanced](/wiki/Super_AMOLED "Super AMOLED") display

* [AURA](/wiki/Motorola_Aura "Motorola Aura") (R) * [FONE](/wiki/Motorola_Fone "Motorola Fone") (F) * [KRZR](/wiki/Motorola_Krzr "Motorola Krzr") (K) * [MING](/wiki/Motorola_Ming "Motorola Ming") (A1680) * [PEBL](/wiki/Motorola_Pebl "Motorola Pebl") (U) * [RAZR](/wiki/Motorola_Razr_V3 "Motorola Razr V3") (V, VE) * [RAZR2](/wiki/Motorola_Razr2 "Motorola Razr2") (V) * [RAZR3](/wiki/Motorola_Razr3 "Motorola Razr3") (V) * [RIZR](/wiki/Motorola_Rizr "Motorola Rizr") (Z) * [ROKR](/wiki/Motorola_Rokr "Motorola Rokr") (E, EM, W, Z, ZN) * [SLVR](/wiki/Motorola_Slvr "Motorola Slvr") (L) * [ZINE](/wiki/Motorola_Zine "Motorola Zine") (ZN)

* [Atrix 4G](/wiki/Motorola_Atrix_4G "Motorola Atrix 4G")   + [2](/wiki/Motorola_Atrix_2 "Motorola Atrix 2")   + [HD](/w/index.php?title=Motorola_Atrix_HD&action=edit&redlink=1 "Motorola Atrix HD (page does not exist)") * [Backflip](/wiki/Motorola_Backflip "Motorola Backflip") * [Calgary](/wiki/Motorola_Calgary "Motorola Calgary") * [Charm](/wiki/Motorola_Charm "Motorola Charm") * [Citrus](/w/index.php?title=Motorola_Citrus&action=edit&redlink=1 "Motorola Citrus (page does not exist)") * [CLIQ / DEXT](/wiki/Motorola_Cliq "Motorola Cliq")   + [XT](/wiki/Motorola_Cliq_XT "Motorola Cliq XT")   + [2](/wiki/Motorola_Cliq "Motorola Cliq") * [Defy](/wiki/Motorola_Defy "Motorola Defy")   + [2021](/w/index.php?title=Motorola_Defy_(2021)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Motorola Defy (2021) (page does not exist)")   + [2](/w/index.php?title=Motorola_Defy_2&action=edit&redlink=1 "Motorola Defy 2 (page does not exist)") * [Devour](/wiki/Motorola_Devour "Motorola Devour") * [Edge](/wiki/Motorola_Edge "Motorola Edge")   + [2020](/wiki/Motorola_Edge_(2020) "Motorola Edge (2020)")   + [2021](/wiki/Motorola_Edge_(2021) "Motorola Edge (2021)")   + [2022](/w/index.php?title=Motorola_Edge_(2022)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Motorola Edge (2022) (page does not exist)")     -

(Motorola XT905/XT906/XT907) is an [Android](/wiki/Android_(operating_system) "Android (operating system)")-based, [4G](/wiki/4G "4G") [LTE](/wiki/Long_Term_Evolution "Long Term Evolution")-capable [smartphone](/wiki/Smartphone "Smartphone") designed by [Motorola](/wiki/Motorola_Mobility "Motorola Mobility") as a smaller successor to the [Droid Razr](/wiki/Droid_Razr "Droid Razr"). It was advertised as "The full screen phone" with thin edges, though it lacked a robust resolution. It came with a light skin of [Android (operating system)](/wiki/Android_(operating_system) "Android (operating system)") for [Verizon Wireless](/wiki/Verizon_Wireless "Verizon Wireless")[[3]](#cite_note-3) (XT907), [SoftBank Mobile](/wiki/SoftBank_Mobile "SoftBank Mobile")[[4]](#cite_note-4) (XT902), and [Telstra](/wiki/Telstra "Telstra") as well as an unbranded retail version for the Australian market[[5]](#cite_note-5) (both XT905). The Electrify M (XT901) for [U.S. Cellular](/wiki/U.S._Cellular "U.S. Cellular") is a [CDMA](/wiki/Code_division_multiple_access "Code division multiple access") handheld with a different housing, but otherwise same specification as the Razr M.[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)
["DROID RAZR M by Motorola Fact Sheet"](http://mediacenter.motorola.com/Content/Detail.aspx?ReleaseID=15251&NewsAreaID=22&ClientID=1). [Motorola](/wiki/Motorola "Motorola"). Sep 5, 2012.

["The full screen phone"](https://web.archive.org/web/20160308052517/http://www.motorola.com/us/Droid-Razr-M/m-DROID-RAZR-M.html). Archived from [the original](http://www.motorola.com/us/Droid-Razr-M/m-DROID-RAZR-M.html) on 8 March 2016. Retrieved 2 December 2013.

["Motorola RAZR M - The Full Screen Phone"](https://web.archive.org/web/20141219063947/http://www.motorola.com.au/consumers/MOTOROLA-RAZR-M/m-Motorola-RAZR-M,en_AU,pd.html). Archived from [the original](http://www.motorola.com.au/consumers/MOTOROLA-RAZR-M/m-Motorola-RAZR-M,en_AU,pd.html) on 19 December 2014. Retrieved 25 December 2014.

["MOTOROLA ELECTRIFY M - Android 4G Smartphone"](https://web.archive.org/web/20160308052517/http://www.motorola.com/us/Droid-Razr-M/m-DROID-RAZR-M.html). Archived from [the original](http://www.motorola.com/us/Droid-Razr-M/m-DROID-RAZR-M.html) on 8 March 2016. Retrieved 25 December 2014.
["MOTOROLA XT901 Electrify M - Full phone specification"](http://www.imei.info/phonedatabase/13273-motorola-xt901-electrify-m/). Retrieved 25 December 2014.

["Verizon Approves Jelly Bean Update 98.12.4 for DROID RAZR M, Starts Rolling Out Today"](http://www.droid-life.com/2012/11/09/verizon-approves-jelly-bean-update-98-12-4-for-the-droid-razr-m-should-start-rolling-out-soon/). *Droid Life*. 9 November 2012. Retrieved 10 November 2012.

["Software update for your DROID RAZR M BY MOTOROLA"](https://web.archive.org/web/20160303231017/http://www.verizonwireless.com/dam/support/pdf/system_update/razrm-05-12-14.pdf) (PDF). *Verizon Wireless*. 12 May 2014. Archived from [the original](http://www.verizonwireless.com/dam/support/pdf/system_update/razrm-05-12-14.pdf) (PDF) on 3 March 2016. Retrieved 25 December 2014.

["The worlds first 2Ghz Phone"](https://web.archive.org/web/20121223185455/http://wetalkworld.net/the-worlds-first-2ghz-phone/953). TalkWorld. Archived from [the original](http://wetalkworld.net/the-worlds-first-2ghz-phone/953) on 23 December 2012. Retrieved 18 September 2012.

The **Droid Razr M** (Motorola XT905/XT906/XT907) is an [Android](/wiki/Android_(operating_system) "Android (operating system)")-based, [4G](/wiki/4G "4G") [LTE](/wiki/Long_Term_Evolution "Long Term Evolution")-capable [smartphone](/wiki/Smartphone "Smartphone") designed by [Motorola](/wiki/Motorola_Mobility "Motorola Mobility") as a smaller successor to the [Droid Razr](/wiki/Droid_Razr "Droid Razr"). It was advertised as "The full screen phone" with thin edges, though it lacked a robust resolution. It came with a light skin of [Android (operating system)](/wiki/Android_(operating_system) "Android (operating system)") for [Verizon Wireless](/wiki/Verizon_Wireless "Verizon Wireless")[[3]](#cite_note-3) (XT907), [SoftBank Mobile](/wiki/SoftBank_Mobile "SoftBank Mobile")[[4]](#cite_note-4) (XT902), and [Telstra](/wiki/Telstra "Telstra") as well as an unbranded retail version for the Australian market[[5]](#cite_note-5) (both XT905). The Electrify M (XT901) for [U.S. Cellular](/wiki/U.S._Cellular "U.S. Cellular") is a [CDMA](/wiki/Code_division_multiple_access "Code division multiple access") handheld with a different housing, but otherwise same specification as the Razr M.[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Droid_Razr_M&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The lower-end Droid Razr M was announced alongside the [Droid Razr HD](/wiki/Droid_Razr_HD "Droid Razr HD") and Razr Maxx HD on 5 September 2012. The Razr HD and M models ship with [Android 4.0 (Ice Cream Sandwich)](/wiki/Android_Ice_Cream_Sandwich "Android Ice Cream Sandwich"). The [Android 4.1 (Jelly Bean)](/wiki/Android_Jelly_Bean "Android Jelly Bean") upgrade for the Razr M started rolling out on Nov 9, 2012[[8]](#cite_note-8) and the upgrade to [Android 4.4 (KitKat)](/wiki/Android_KitKat "Android KitKat") on May 12, 2014.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## RAZR i

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Droid_Razr_M&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: RAZR i")]

The [RAZR i](/wiki/Motorola_RAZR_i "Motorola RAZR i") (XT890) is an almost identical edition except it has an [Intel Atom](/wiki/Intel_Atom "Intel Atom") Z2460 processor. This means it has more processing power, but less imaging power; however, it lacks [LTE](/wiki/LTE_(telecommunication) "LTE (telecommunication)") support.[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12)
